sourceMovie.title: Anyone But You
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Anyone But You works as a romance movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The romance and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Amélie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Anyone But You's romance stayed with you, Amélie (2001) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The comedy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Pretty Woman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Anyone But You excels in romance, Pretty Woman (1990) doubles down on comedy to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: 10 Things I Hate About You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: 10 Things I Hate About You (1999) is the natural next step after Anyone But You: same romance DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and comedy elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Lost in Translation
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Anyone But You who respond to its romance will find Lost in Translation (2003) immediately familiar. Both films share a comedy sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Notting Hill
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Anyone But You fans keep landing on Notting Hill (1999) — the romance is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: The Kissing Booth 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Kissing Booth 2 draws from the same romance well as Anyone But You, channeling comedy in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2020 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The DUFF
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The DUFF (2015) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Anyone But You later expanded. If Anyone But You resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: He's Just Not That Into You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The romance appeal of Anyone But You — that funny edge, the comedy — translates cleanly into He's Just Not That Into You (2009). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: No Hard Feelings
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind No Hard Feelings (2023) clearly understood what makes romance work — the same quality that keeps Anyone But You rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Chocolat
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Chocolat (2000) is the kind of film Anyone But You fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The romance overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

faq.question: What should I watch after Anyone But You?
faq.answer: Try Amélie, Pretty Woman, 10 Things I Hate About You—each shares DNA with Anyone But You while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Anyone But You?
faq.answer: Anyone But You (2023) is best described as Romance, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Anyone But You worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
